name: Stipendium Hungaricum Scholarship
funding: Full tuition waiver + monthly stipend ~HUF 43,700 (~$120 USD) + free dormitory accommodation + health insurance
description: The Stipendium Hungaricum programme is one of the largest scholarship programmes in Hungary, funded by the Hungarian Government. It enables talented international students to pursue their studies at Hungarian higher education institutions. Bangladesh is one of the eligible sending countries with a strong annual quota.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students must apply through the University Grants Commission (UGC) of Bangladesh. The UGC releases the application portal every November. IELTS is NOT required — a Medium of Instruction (MOI) letter from your university is accepted. Contact UGC at ugc@ugc.gov.bd for the application portal link.

name: Türkiye Burslari Scholarship
funding: Full tuition + monthly stipend (800–1,400 TRY) + free accommodation + health insurance + one-time language course
bd_sending_partner: Direct online application via YTB portal
description: Türkiye Burslari (Turkey Scholarships) is awarded by the Presidency for Turks Abroad and Related Communities (YTB) to international students for bachelor's, master's, and doctoral programmes at Turkish universities. It is one of the most popular scholarships among Bangladeshi students.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply directly through the online YTB portal — no local agent is needed. Turkish language course is provided for free before the academic year. CGPA cut-off is 75% for Bachelor's, 75% for Master's, and 75% for PhD. IELTS not required — Turkish universities accept MOI or conduct their own English assessment.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters
funding: Full tuition + €1,400/month living allowance + travel allowance + health insurance
description: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters are prestigious European Union-funded programmes where students study in two or more European countries, earning a joint or double degree. The scholarship is highly competitive but comes with generous financial support. Each year, dozens of Bangladeshi students receive this scholarship.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi applicants are eligible under the 'Third Country National' category. Apply directly to the specific Erasmus Mundus programme consortium — not through UGC. Each programme has its own deadline and IELTS requirement (typically 6.5). A strong SOP and research background are crucial for selection.

name: DAAD Scholarships (Germany)
funding: Monthly stipend €934 (Master) / €1,200 (PhD) + travel allowance + health insurance + language course allowance
description: The German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) offers a wide range of scholarship programmes for international students and researchers in Germany. Germany has many fully-funded English-taught master's programmes with no tuition fees. DAAD is highly reputed among Bangladeshi science and engineering graduates.
bd_specific_notes: Most German public universities charge NO tuition fees. DAAD scholarship adds a monthly stipend on top. Some programmes accept IELTS 6.0. Bangladeshi students should also note that German student visa requires proof of blocked account (€11,208/year). DAAD scholarship covers this requirement.

name: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)
funding: Full tuition waiver + free accommodation + monthly stipend CNY 2,500–3,500 (~$350–$480 USD) + comprehensive medical insurance
bd_sending_partner: Ministry of Education (MoE) Bangladesh / Bangladesh Embassy in Beijing
description: The Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC) is funded by the Ministry of Education of China to support international students studying in Chinese universities. It is one of the most popular fully-funded scholarships for Bangladeshi students due to no IELTS requirement and generous quota for Bangladesh.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladesh has a dedicated quota under the CSC programme. Students can apply via Type A (through Chinese Embassy in Dhaka), Type B (through the Chinese university directly), or through Bangladesh Ministry of Education. No IELTS required — Chinese universities accept MOI or their own English tests. Contact: Chinese Embassy Dhaka, Road 2, Baridhara Diplomatic Enclave.

name: Korean Government Scholarship (KGSP)
funding: Full tuition + monthly stipend KRW 900,000–1,000,000 (~$680–$760 USD) + airfare + Korean language training (1 year) + health insurance + settlement allowance
bd_sending_partner: Bangladesh Embassy in Seoul / Ministry of Education Bangladesh
description: The Global Korea Scholarship (GKS/KGSP) is provided by the National Institute for International Education (NIIED) under Korea's Ministry of Education to promote international education in the Republic of Korea. Bangladesh is among the top recipient countries with a significant annual quota.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students can apply through the Korean Embassy in Dhaka (Embassy Track) or directly through a Korean university (University Track). A one-year Korean language program is mandatory before the degree starts. IELTS not required — TOPIK (Korean proficiency) score is advantageous but not mandatory for English-medium programmes.

name: MEXT Japanese Government Scholarship
funding: Full tuition + monthly stipend ¥117,000–¥144,000 (~$780–$960 USD) + airfare (round trip) + no tuition fee
description: The MEXT (Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology) Scholarship is offered by the Japanese government to international students for undergraduate, graduate, and research studies in Japan. Bangladesh is an eligible country with an annual quota offered through the Japanese Embassy in Dhaka.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply through the Japanese Embassy in Dhaka. The application process includes a written exam (EJU or university-specific test), document screening, and an interview. Japanese language courses are provided. English-taught programmes exist for graduate studies. No IELTS required for Embassy track — Japanese universities may require it for University track.

name: Commonwealth Scholarships (UK)
funding: Full tuition + monthly living allowance + airfare (economy) + warm clothing allowance + thesis grant + study travel grant
description: Commonwealth Scholarships are funded by the UK Government through the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO). These prestigious awards are for students from Commonwealth countries, including Bangladesh, to pursue Master's or PhD studies at UK universities. Bangladesh receives a significant annual quota.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students must apply through UGC Bangladesh (nominating body). The UGC screens applications before forwarding to the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission. Strong academic records (First Class Bachelor's equivalent), a compelling development impact statement, and excellent references are crucial. IELTS minimum 6.5 typically required.

name: Chevening Scholarships (UK)
funding: Full tuition + monthly living allowance (£1,196/month in London, £1,043/month elsewhere) + economy return airfare + visa fee
description: Chevening is the UK government's international awards programme, funded by the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO). It offers full scholarships to study any Master's degree at any UK university for future leaders with at least 2 years of work experience. Bangladesh is a major recipient country.
bd_specific_notes: Chevening requires minimum 2 YEARS of full-time work experience (part-time not counted). Bangladeshi applicants apply to the British High Commission in Dhaka. The selection process involves online application, interview, and employer reference. IELTS 6.5 (or equivalent) required before receiving the award — conditional offers are given first.

name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program (USA)
funding: Full tuition + J-1 visa sponsorship + monthly stipend + airfare + health insurance + English language training if needed
description: The Fulbright Program is the U.S. Government's flagship international educational exchange program sponsored by the U.S. Department of State. The Fulbright Foreign Student Program enables graduate students, young professionals, and artists from abroad to study and conduct research in the United States.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply through the American Center Dhaka (part of the US Embassy). The process involves online application, interviews at US Embassy, and final selection by Fulbright selection committee. GRE may be required for some disciplines. TOEFL or IELTS required. Candidates with research experience and clear academic goals are preferred.

name: Romanian Government Scholarship (MFA)
funding: Full tuition waiver + monthly grant €65 + free accommodation in student dormitories + one-year Romanian language preparatory course
bd_sending_partner: Romanian Embassy Dhaka / Ministry of Foreign Affairs Romania
description: The Romanian Government, through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), offers scholarships to citizens of non-EU countries, including Bangladesh. These scholarships cover full tuition and basic accommodation at Romanian public universities. The programme has been popular among Bangladeshi students for medical, engineering, and arts degrees.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply through the Romanian Embassy in Dhaka or online via the MFA portal. A Romanian language preparatory year (free of cost) is mandatory before the degree programme begins. IELTS not required — instruction is in Romanian (after the language year) or English for select programmes. contact: Romanian Embassy Dhaka, House 34, Road 5, Sector 6, Uttara.

name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
funding: Monthly stipend CHF 1,920 (~$2,100 USD) + tuition exemption + health insurance + accommodation allowance + airfare
description: The Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships are awarded by the Swiss Confederation to promote international exchange and research cooperation. These prestigious scholarships support postgraduate researchers and artists from over 180 countries, including Bangladesh, to study at Swiss federal institutes and universities.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi applicants with research background apply through the Swiss Embassy in Dhaka. For postdoctoral research, candidates must have a prior agreement from a Swiss university professor/supervisor. The scholarship is extremely competitive — PhD holders or candidates with strong research publications are preferred. IELTS 6.5+ or German/French proficiency required.

name: Australia Awards Scholarships
funding: Full tuition + living allowance AUD 35,013/year + establishment allowance + health cover (OSHC) + airfare + pre-course English training
bd_sending_partner: Australia Awards Bangladesh (AAB) / Australian High Commission Dhaka
description: Australia Awards Scholarships are funded by the Australian Government's Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT). For Bangladesh, they target students and professionals in priority areas including governance, infrastructure, and human development. Bangladesh is among the top recipient countries in South Asia.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply through Australia Awards Bangladesh (AAB) in Dhaka. Priority is given to candidates from outside Dhaka/Chittagong and those from disadvantaged backgrounds. Work experience (2+ years) is highly preferred. Pre-departure English training is provided if needed. Contact AAB Office: Gulshan 2, Dhaka.

name: Italian Government Scholarship (MAECI)
funding: Monthly grant €900 + free health insurance + waiver of application fees (tuition fees may vary by university — many Italian public universities have very low fees)
description: The Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) offers scholarships to international students for study, research, and professional training in Italy. Bangladesh is an eligible country. Many Italian universities offer English-taught programmes, and the country offers excellent STEM and arts education.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply via the studyinitaly.esteri.it portal. Applications are submitted online and then verified by the Italian Embassy in Dhaka. IELTS not generally required for the scholarship itself — individual university English requirements apply. Italian language course (basic) is encouraged. Deadlines vary slightly each year.

name: Slovak Government Scholarship
funding: Monthly stipend €350 (Master) / €420 (PhD) + exemption from tuition fees at public universities + health insurance
bd_sending_partner: Slovak Embassy in New Delhi (covers Bangladesh) / SAIA Slovakia
description: The Slovak Academic Information Agency (SAIA) administers scholarships funded by the Slovak Government under bilateral agreements with foreign countries. Bangladesh citizens are eligible to apply for Master's and PhD programmes at Slovak public universities. The scholarship is an underrated gem with lower competition than Western European scholarships.
bd_specific_notes: Bangladeshi students apply online through the SAIA portal. Applications are reviewed by the Slovak Embassy in New Delhi (which covers Bangladesh). English-taught programmes are available at several Slovak universities including STU Bratislava, Comenius University, and Slovak University of Agriculture. IELTS not required — Slovak universities accept MOI letters.
